what: imuGAP: an Estimation Model for Measles Vaccination Coverage
title: imuGAP: an Estimation Model for Measles Vaccination Coverage
text:
name imuGAP stands “Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection”, name implies, package provides stan-based model estimating measles vaccination coverage location age cohort. core model represents target population life-long propensity vaccination; proportion, ϕ\phi, population unlikely vaccinate complementary proportion, 1−ϕ1 - \phi, likely vaccinate. population experiences vaccination rate, λ\lambda, model time eras, according vaccination eligibility schedule, ν
u. core parameters can vary time location, user-specifiable way. Focusing just core model element, imagine particular population location ii cohort aa (aa denotes start time period group born). cohort now age tt, vaccine schedule first dose ν(t)
u(t), expected fraction group least one dose : P(≥1 dose)=(1−ϕi,)(1−exp{−∫atλi,(s)ν(s)ds}) P(\ge	extrm{1 dose}) = \left(1 - \phi_{, }ight) \left(1 - \exp\left\{-\int_a^{t} \lambda_{, }(s)
u(s) d	extrm{s}ight\}ight) say, representing vaccination coverage via survival model. Leaving aside “unlikely” population

title: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage
text: imuGAP sampler fits two model effects: lifetime tendency towards vaccination time varying force vaccination. fit effects, model evaluates observations may combine many populations. model distinguishes populations place, birth timing, age. model organizes places hierarchy, example state contains counties turn contain cities. effects particular place combine impact enclosing locations.

title: Canonicalize Location Data — canonicalize_locations
text: data.table, : "id", "parent_id" columns originally supplied, possibly reordered "c_id", "cp_id" columns, canonicalized id/parent_id columns, representing order used sampler "layer" column, integer 1 (root), 2 (root children), 3 (grandchildren), etc

title: Canonicalize Location Data — canonicalize_locations
text: [imuGAP()] sampler works hierarchical model locations, must provided structure. method checks location structure validity, returns canonical version including layer membership. valid structure : unique root, cycles, duplicate ids Users may explicitly identify root id providing row parent_id equal NA. Otherwise, parent_id appear id treated root. input valid, method create canonicalized version. version, ids run 1:N, N number distinct ids. order determined layer order, position parent within layer, "natural" order (.e., whatever base R sort() yields).

text: observations [data.frame()], observed data, least three columns: "id" column; type, long unique, non-NA "positive" column; non-negative integers, observed number vaccinated individuals "sample_n" column; positive integers, number individuals sampled, must greater equal "positive" optionally, "censored" column; numeric, NA (uncensored) 1 (right-censored);

title: Canonicalize Observation Data — canonicalize_observations
text: canonical observation object, [data.table()] : "c_id" column, integer sequence 1; order observations passed estimation original "id" column, possibly reordered "positive" "sample_n" columns, possibly reordered "censored" column; NA, present original observations argument

title: Canonicalize Observation Data — canonicalize_observations
text: observations object documents observations used fit model. Conceptually, row represents observation vaccination status within population. population need uniform (see [canonicalize_populations()]) concerning single cohort time: observation generally best available resolution data. resolution can vary across rows. [imuGAP()] sampler uses information resolutions automatically figure compare latent process model different observations. optional "censored" column: model supports vaccination status indicators vaccine specific well represent individual set vaccines (including target vaccine). specific coverage target vaccine right-censored latter case: -coverage minimum coverage target. least data censored, must supply "censored" column correctly estimate coverage. Mark uncensored observations NA, right-censored observations 1. Note "0" valid value time; preserving potential future support left-censoring.
